articles.title: Haven’t Booked a Summer Trip Yet? These Are the 10 Best Choices for 2026
articles.content:
不知道有没有人需要听这句话，但七月还没预订任何夏季旅行，并不意味着你错过了出行的机会。每年我都会告诉自己旅行计划已经完成了，然后突然发现日历上还有几个周末空着，或者某个目的地吸引了我的目光。不知不觉间，我就在比较航班了，还说服自己再来一次周末小长假完全合理。好消息是？如果你还没预订夏季假期，现在仍然有足够的时间。

根据Expedia最新发布的《2026年夏季目的地》报告，美国有多个目的地值得你这个季节列入旅行计划。榜单涵盖了阳光海滩度假胜地、户外探险中心和迷人的小城镇。如果你希望省心省力，来一次自驾游或短途飞行，那就有福了——Expedia发现，国内那些开车可达或飞机短程即到的目的地，正是今年夏天游客蜂拥而至的地方。

继续往下看，了解哪个目的地荣登榜首，以及它为什么也应该在你的清单上。

这不仅仅挑选几个风景优美的度假胜地那么简单。Expedia通过比较2026年5月22日至8月31日期间与2025年同期住宿搜索数据，来分析哪些地方兴趣增长最强劲。他们还深入研究了社交媒体上的讨论，看看今年美国哪些目的地最热门。

为了使调查更全面，Expedia与市场调研公司OnePoll合作，对九个国家的11000名旅行者进行了关于度假习惯的调查。结果就是这张2026年夏季美国最令人期待和预订最多的目的地快照。

请准备好……第一名是犹他州的圣乔治。这座沙漠城市在住宿搜索量同比增长中跃居首位——确切地说，增长了125%。

虽然许多游客将这座城市作为附近锡安国家公园的出发点，但圣乔治本身就有很多值得留下来的理由。比如：风景如画的徒步小径、壮观的沙漠景观、被绝美景色环绕的高尔夫球场，以及日益蓬勃的美食文化。此外，你可以在上午探索红色岩石小径——却不必承受西部一些更热门度假胜地的拥挤（和高昂价格）。

我从未去过圣乔治，但在游览过莫阿布之后，我理解为什么犹他州南部拥有如此忠实的粉丝群。矗立的红色岩石拱门和壮观的大峡谷景色，让人感觉仿佛踏上了另一个星球。（难怪美国宇航局在犹他州的沙漠中测试太空漫游车并训练宇航员。）

和莫阿布一样，圣乔治让你无需全程开车就能探索很多地方。从斯诺峡谷州立公园开始，那里火红的悬崖、古老的熔岩流、化石沙丘和风景优美的小径，让你感觉就像在镇外发现了一片小型沙漠。想要一次付出少回报大的轻松冒险，可以去先驱者公园和迪克西岩，在那里你可以在红色岩石间攀爬，俯瞰圣乔治的全景。

徒步一天后想凉快一下？可以到沙坑州立公园游泳、划皮划艇或放松休息。然后探索红崖国家保护区，那里的步道穿过色彩斑斓的岩石构造和宁静的风景，完美展示了这片犹他州土地为何如此令人难忘。

如果犹他州的圣乔治还没有出现在你的夏季愿望清单上（目前还没有的话），Expedia认为还有很多其他值得你关注的目的地。以下是Expedia报告中排名靠前的其他美国目的地。

塔科马位于西雅图以南，是探索太平洋西北地区一些最令人惊叹风景的绝佳大本营。如果你热爱户外活动，可以轻松驱车前往雷尼尔山国家公园，在野花丛中和冰川景观中徒步。或者去奥林匹克国家公园，那里有崎岖的海岸线、森林和瀑布。别错过塔科马点迪芬斯公园，这是一个滨水休闲区，拥有小径和普吉特海湾的美景，还有玻璃博物馆可以让你感受这座城市的创意一面。最近的主要机场是西雅图-塔科马国际机场（SEA），大约30分钟车程。

阿什维尔是那种上午追逐山景、下午穿梭于啤酒厂的地方。沿着蓝岭公路欣赏风景，或者探索比特莫庄园广阔的花园和建筑，这是美国最大的私人住宅。当你想用美食和美酒来感受这座城市时，阿什维尔的河滨艺术区和热闹的市中心到处都是当地餐厅、手工啤酒厂和独特的个性。阿什维尔地区机场（AVL）让前往非常方便。

如果你理想的夏季旅行是阳光、水和慢节奏，布雷登顿满足所有条件。这个墨西哥湾沿岸城市是安娜玛利亚岛的门户，那里有着洁白的沙滩、碧绿的海水和老佛罗里达的魅力。在科奇纳海滩放松一天，或者探索罗宾逊自然保护区，那里的步道和水路使其成为划船和观赏野生动物的热门之地。最近的机场是萨拉索塔-布雷登顿国际机场（SRQ），只有很短的车程。

圣克鲁斯是经典加州海滩度假胜地的典范，拥有悠闲的冲浪文化、海景和满满的怀旧风情。花一天时间在著名的圣克鲁斯海滩栈道上玩耍，然后去蒸汽道看冲浪者逐浪，那是这座城市最具标志性的冲浪点之一。想要感受城市更安静的一面，可以漫步自然桥州海滩，那里以壮观的海岸景观和潮汐池闻名。最近的机场是圣何塞国际机场（SJC），大约45分钟车程。

锡耶斯塔礁以其粉末般的白色石英砂而闻名，是一个适合什么都不做、却享受每一分钟的海滩目的地。在锡耶斯塔海滩清澈的墨西哥湾海水中游泳，或者探索附近的乌龟海滩区域，感受更放松的氛围。当太阳开始下山时，前往锡耶斯塔礁村，那里有很多餐厅、现场音乐和经典海滩小镇的氛围。最近的机场是萨拉索塔-布雷登顿国际机场（SRQ）。

谁不喜欢一次很棒的冒险呢？特拉基是太浩湖最佳的全年度假基地，户外活动几乎无穷无尽。夏天可以沿着唐纳湖周围的小径徒步，在太浩湖游泳或划皮划艇度过一天，或者探索迷人的历史市中心，那里有当地商店和餐厅。最近的主要机场是里诺-太浩湖国际机场（RNO），大约45分钟车程。

博兹曼是那种让你想尽可能多待在户外的目的地。它是黄石国家公园的热门出发点。离城镇更近的地方，可以在海莱特峡谷的风景步道上徒步，或者漫步于博兹曼市中心的餐厅、啤酒厂和精品店之间。博兹曼黄石国际机场（BZN）就位于城市外，交通便利。

普雷斯科特展现了亚利桑那州更凉爽、更出人意料的一面，那里有松林和山景。在沃森湖边散步，那里的花岗岩巨砾和碧蓝湖水别具特色，或者探索拇指堡，那是该地区最著名的地标之一，从山顶可以俯瞰全景。最近的机场是普雷斯科特地区机场（PRC），不过你也可以飞往凤凰城天港国际机场（PHX）然后开车前往。

这座新英格兰城市将历史魅力与出人意料的活力艺术和美食场景完美融合。沿着普罗维登斯河畔漫步，欣赏城市著名的“水上火焰”活动，或者探索布朗大学附近大学山社区色彩斑斓的街道。自认为是个美食家？可以去联邦山看看，那里以意大利餐厅和当地美食著称。最近的机场是罗德岛T.F.格林国际机场（PVD），距离市中心只有几分钟路程。

这篇《还没预订夏季旅行？以下是2026年最值得选择的10个目的地》文章首次发表于《读者文摘》。
articles.summary:
I don’t know who needs to hear this, but having zero summer trips booked in July doesn’t mean you’ve missed your chance to get away. Every year, I tell myself I’m done planning vacations, and then I realize there are still a few weekends left on the calendar or a destination catches my eye. Before I know it, I’m comparing flights and convincing myself that one more weekend getaway is totally justified. The good news? If you haven’t booked your summer vacation yet, there’s still plenty of time.

According to Expedia’s new 2026 Destinations of the Summer report, a handful of U.S. destinations deserve a spot on your travel radar this season. The list features everything from sunny beach escapes and outdoor-adventure hubs to charming smaller cities. And if you’re hoping to keep things easy with a road trip or a quick flight, you’re in luck—Expedia found that domestic destinations within driving distance or a short hop on a plane are the places travelers are flocking to this summer.

Read on to see which destination claimed the No. 1 spot and why it should be on your list too.

Get Reader’s Digest’s Read Up newsletter for more travel news, fun facts, humor, cleaning and tech all week long.

How did the report determine the best summer travel destinations in the U.S.?

It wasn’t just a matter of picking a few pretty vacation spots. Expedia looked at where interest is surging the most by comparing accommodation searches for trips between May 22 and Aug. 31, 2026, with the same summer travel period in 2025. It also dug into social media conversations to see which U.S. destinations everyone has been talking about this year.

To round things out, Expedia partnered with the market research firm OnePoll, which surveyed 11,000 travelers across nine countries about their vacation habits. The result? A snapshot of the U.S. destinations that are generating the most excitement and bookings for summer 2026.

What is the No. 1 summer travel destination for 2026?

Drumroll, please … it’s St. George, Utah. The desert city took the top spot after seeing the biggest jump in accommodation searches compared to last year—by 125% percent, to be exact.

While many visitors use the city as a jumping-off point for nearby Zion National Park, St. George has plenty of reasons to stick around. Think: scenic hiking trails, dramatic desert views, golf courses surrounded by stunning landscapes and a growing food scene. Plus, you can spend the morning exploring red-rock trails—all without the crowds (or price tags) of some of the West’s bigger vacation hot spots.

What are the best things to do in St. George, Utah?

I’ve never visited St. George, but after visiting Moab, I understand why southern Utah has such a loyal fan club. Between the towering red-rock arches and dramatic canyon views, it feels like stepping onto another planet. (No wonder NASA tests its space rovers and trains its astronauts in the Utah desert.)

Like Moab, St. George gives you plenty to explore without having to spend your entire trip in the car. Start with Snow Canyon State Park, where fiery red cliffs, ancient lava flows, petrified sand dunes and scenic trails make it feel like a mini desert right outside town. For an easy adventure with a big payoff, head to Pioneer Park and Dixie Rock, where you can scramble around the red rocks and take in sweeping views of St. George.

Want to cool off after a day of hiking? You can swim, kayak or relax at Sand Hollow State Park. Then, explore Red Cliffs National Conservation Area, where trails lead through colorful rock formations and peaceful landscapes that showcase exactly why this part of Utah is so unforgettable.

What are the other best summer destinations in the U.S.?

If St. George, Utah, isn’t on your summer bucket list (yet), there are plenty of other destinations Expedia says should be on your radar. Here are the other U.S. destinations that earned top billing on Expedia’s report.

Tacoma, Washington

Located just south of Seattle, Tacoma makes a great home base for exploring some of the Pacific Northwest’s most jaw-dropping scenery. If you love basking in the outdoors, you can easily make the drive to Mount Rainier National Park for hikes surrounded by wildflowers and glacier views. Or head to Olympic National Park for rugged coastlines, forests and waterfalls. Don’t miss spots like Point Defiance Park, a waterfront escape with trails and views of Puget Sound, and the Museum of Glass for a dose of the city’s creative side. The closest major airport is Seattle-Tacoma International Airport (SEA), about a 30-minute drive away.

Asheville, North Carolina

Asheville is the kind of place where you can spend the morning chasing mountain views and the afternoon hopping between breweries. Take in the scenery along the Blue Ridge Parkway, or explore the sprawling gardens and architecture of the Biltmore Estate, America’s largest home. When you’re ready to eat and drink your way through town, Asheville’s River Arts District and lively downtown are filled with local restaurants, craft breweries and plenty of personality. Asheville Regional Airport (AVL) makes getting there easy.

Bradenton, Florida

If your ideal summer trip involves sunshine, water and a slower pace, Bradenton checks all the boxes. This Gulf Coast city is the gateway to Anna Maria Island, where you’ll find sugary beaches, turquoise water and old-Florida charm. Spend the day relaxing at Coquina Beach or explore the natural beauty of Robinson Preserve, where trails and waterways make it a favorite for kayaking and wildlife spotting. The closest airport is Sarasota-Bradenton International Airport (SRQ), which is only a short drive away.

Santa Cruz, California

Santa Cruz is the definition of a classic California beach getaway, with a laid-back surf scene, ocean views and loads of nostalgic charm. Spend the day riding the famous Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk, then watch surfers catch waves at Steamer Lane, one of the city’s most iconic surf spots. For a quieter side of town, take a stroll through Natural Bridges State Beach, known for its dramatic coastal views and tide pools. The closest airport is San Jose International Airport (SJC), about a 45-minute drive away.

Siesta Key, Florida

Famous for its powdery white-quartz sand, Siesta Key feels like the kind of beach destination made for doing absolutely nothing—and enjoying every minute of it. Spend your days swimming in the clear Gulf waters at Siesta Beach, or explore the nearby Turtle Beach area for a more relaxed vibe. When the sun starts setting, head to Siesta Key Village for restaurants, live music and a classic beach-town atmosphere. Sarasota-Bradenton International Airport (SRQ) is the closest airport.

Truckee, California

Who doesn’t love a good ol’ adventure? Truckee is the best year-round basecamp for Lake Tahoe, where outdoor activities are practically endless. In summer, hike trails around Donner Lake, spend the day swimming or kayaking at Lake Tahoe, or explore the charming historic downtown filled with local shops and restaurants. The closest major airport is Reno-Tahoe International Airport (RNO), about a 45-minute drive away.

Bozeman, Montana

Bozeman is the kind of destination that makes you want to spend as much time outside as possible. It’s a popular jumping-off point for Yellowstone National Park. Closer to town, hike the scenic trails at Hyalite Canyon, or stroll through downtown Bozeman’s restaurants, breweries and boutiques. Bozeman Yellowstone International Airport (BZN) is conveniently located just outside the city.

Prescott, Arizona

Prescott offers a cooler, more unexpected side of Arizona with pine forests and mountain views. Take a walk around Watson Lake, known for its unique granite boulders and bright blue water, or explore Thumb Butte, one of the area’s most recognizable landmarks with panoramic views from the top. The closest airport is Prescott Regional Airport (PRC), though you can also fly into Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport (PHX) and make the drive.

Providence, Rhode Island

This New England city blends historic charm with a surprisingly lively arts and food scene. Take a walk along the Providence Riverwalk, catch the city’s famous WaterFire event or explore the colorful streets of the College Hill neighborhood near Brown University. Consider yourself a foodie? Check out Federal Hill, known for its Italian restaurants and local favorites. The closest airport is Rhode Island T.F. Green International Airport (PVD), just minutes from downtown.

Sources:

  • Expedia: “Where to Travel This Summer: Closer-to-Home Getaways Dominate Expedia’s Destinations of the Summer List”

The No. 1 Travel Destination for 2026

These August Days Are the Cheapest Days to Fly

Airlines with Cheap Deals Like Spirit

The post Haven’t Booked a Summer Trip Yet? These Are the 10 Best Choices for 2026 appeared first on Reader's Digest.

articles.title: This Is the Most Popular Grilled Food in Each State
articles.content:
有什么比在阳光明媚的日子与亲朋好友围坐在户外、烤架生起、汉堡牛排和烧烤的诱人香气弥漫空气中更惬意的事呢？烧烤是美国最受欢迎的休闲活动之一，约65%的家庭拥有户外烤架，这就不难理解为什么了。牛排落在烤架上的滋滋声和第一口美味的期待，总是令人难以抗拒。

虽然我们中的许多人都会选择多汁汉堡和纯正美式热狗这样的经典美食，但你可能惊讶地发现，各州的烧烤口味各不相同。根据Innerbody最近的一项研究，美国人并不是都在烤架上烤同样的食物。有些地方对鸡翅情有独钟，有些则喜欢烤牛肉片，还有些地方出人意料地热爱烤洋葱圈（说实话，这可能需要加入我的下次烧烤菜单）。

继续阅读，了解每个州最受欢迎的烧烤食物，看看你的烧烤首选是否榜上有名。

为了找出美国人最喜欢的烧烤食物，Innerbody分析了50个州和华盛顿特区的谷歌趋势搜索数据。研究团队从一个庞大的烧烤美食清单开始，搜索了“烤肋眼牛排”和“玉米要烤多久”等关键词，以确保结果反映的是真正的烧烤兴趣，而不仅仅是普通食物渴望。

在此基础上，他们将范围缩小到57种流行烧烤食物，涵盖牛肉、鸡肉、猪肉、海鲜、水果和蔬菜等类别，以找出每个州的最爱。

并非每个州都以汉堡闻名！以下是美国人在烤架上放置的食物。

虽然汉堡在全国范围内排名第一，成为最受关注的烧烤食物，但牛排在全美大部分地区同样引人注目。研究发现，17个州将牛排列为首选，超过其他所有烧烤食物，表明许多美国人渴望一块完美烤制的牛肉。

紧随其后的是鸡胸肉，显示出美国人對另一种经典烧烤美食的持续热爱。海鲜爱好者也明确表达了他们的偏好，虾和龙虾成为最受欢迎的烤海鲜选择，而玉米则在农产品类别中拔得头筹。

明尼苏达州荣登全美最热爱烧烤的州榜首，其次是威斯康星州和南卡罗来纳州。明尼苏达州对烧烤的热爱可能让一些人意外，但该州在温暖月份以户外生活方式著称。

邻州威斯康星州的强劲表现也合情合理，这里以热爱后院烧烤和啤酒香肠闻名。与此同时，南卡罗来纳州的上榜可能与其深厚的烧烤传统有关，慢烤肉类和户外烹饪已融入该州的饮食文化。

是的，确实有不少惊喜！最大的意外是看到菠萝作为唯一的烤水果上榜。我承认，我从未想过把菠萝放到烤架上，但它的天然甜味和多汁口感显然能创造出难以抗拒的烟熏焦糖风味组合。

玉米也在这个烧烤季掀起热潮。在分析夏季前三周的搜索数据后，Innerbody发现玉米是23个州增长最快的烧烤食物，包括加利福尼亚州、纽约州、宾夕法尼亚州和威斯康星州。

但或许最出人意料的发现是德克萨斯州——这个以烧烤和肉类闻名的州——竟然位列烧烤兴趣最低的州之一。更令人惊讶的是，烤洋葱在那里拔得头筹！谁能想到呢？！

这篇文章《每个州最受欢迎的烧烤食物》首次发表于Reader's Digest。
articles.summary:
Is there anything better than kicking back outside on a sunny day with your favorite people nearby, the grill fired up and the irresistible smell of burgers, steaks and barbecue filling the air? Grilling is one of America’s favorite pastimes, and with outdoor grills in about 65% of households, it’s easy to see why. There’s just something about that sizzle of a steak hitting the grill and the anticipation of that first delicious bite.

While many of us reach for classic favorites like juicy burgers and all-American hot dogs, you might be surprised to learn that grilling tastes vary from state to state. According to a recent study from Innerbody, Americans aren’t all putting the same foods on their grills. Some places can’t get enough of chicken wings, others fire up the grill for carne asada and some even have a surprising love for grilled onion rings (which, honestly, might need to make their way onto my next cookout menu).

Read on for the most popular grilled food in every state, and see if your go-to grill order made the list.

Get Reader’s Digest’s Read Up newsletter for more food news, fun facts, humor, cleaning, travel and tech all week long.

How did this report determine the most popular grilled food in every state?

To uncover America’s favorite grilled foods, Innerbody analyzed Google Trends search data from the 50 states and Washington, D.C. The team started with a huge list of grilling favorites and looked at searches like “grilled rib eye” and “how long to grill corn” to make sure the results captured true grilling interest—not just general food cravings.

From there, they narrowed down the list to 57 popular grilled foods across categories like beef, chicken, pork, seafood, fruits and vegetables to find each state’s top pick.

What is the most popular grilled food in each state?

Not every state is known for burgers! Here’s what Americans are slapping on the grill.

  • Alabama: Chicken wings
  • Alaska: Flank steak
  • Arizona: Carne asada
  • Arkansas: Burgers
  • California: Tri-tip
  • Colorado: Rib-eye steak
  • Connecticut: Cod
  • Delaware: New York strip
  • Florida: Chicken wings
  • Georgia: Porterhouse
  • Hawaii: Shrimp
  • Idaho: Pineapple
  • Illinois: Skirt steak
  • Indiana: Chicken breast
  • Iowa: Chicken breast
  • Kansas: Mushrooms
  • Kentucky: Peppers
  • Louisiana: Tuna steak
  • Maine: Sausage
  • Maryland: Flank steak
  • Massachusetts: Sausage
  • Michigan: Burgers
  • Minnesota: Green beans
  • Mississippi: Chicken wings
  • Missouri: Chicken wings
  • Montana: Rib-eye steak
  • Nebraska: Zucchini
  • Nevada: Tri-tip
  • New Hampshire: Sausage
  • New Jersey: Filet mignon
  • New Mexico: Rib-eye steak
  • New York: Skirt steak
  • North Carolina: Hot dogs
  • North Dakota: Burgers
  • Ohio: Chicken breast
  • Oklahoma: Chicken legs
  • Oregon: Flank steak
  • Pennsylvania: Hot dogs
  • Rhode Island: Cod
  • South Carolina: Oysters
  • South Dakota: Zucchini
  • Tennessee: Chicken wings
  • Texas: Onions
  • Utah: Carne asada
  • Vermont: Sausage
  • Virginia: Tuna steak
  • Washington: Flank steak
  • West Virginia: Eggplant
  • Wisconsin: Burgers
  • Wyoming: Steak

Was there a clear winner across the country?

While burgers ranked No. 1 nationwide as the top grilled food of interest, steak stole the spotlight across much of the country. The study found that 17 states preferred a steak cut over any other grilled food, showing that plenty of Americans crave a perfectly grilled piece of beef.

Following close behind were chicken breasts, highlighting Americans’ continued love for another classic grilling favorite. Seafood fans also made their preferences known, with shrimp and lobster emerging as the top grilled seafood choices, while corn claimed the top spot among produce.

What states love grilling the most?

Minnesota came in first for the biggest grilling fans in the country, followed by Wisconsin and South Carolina. Minnesota’s love for the grill might come as a surprise to some, but the state is known for its outdoorsy vibes during warm-weather months.

Neighboring Wisconsin’s strong showing also makes sense, with its famous love for backyard cookouts and bratwurst. Meanwhile, South Carolina’s spot on the list may be tied to its deep barbecue traditions, where slow-cooked meats and outdoor cooking are woven into the state’s food culture.

Were there any big surprises?

Yes, quite a few! The biggest surprise was seeing pineapple on this list as the sole grilled fruit. I’ll admit, I’ve never thought to throw pineapple on the grill, but its natural sweetness and juicy texture apparently make a smoky, caramelized flavor combination that’s hard to resist.

Corn also made a major splash this grilling season. After analyzing search data from the three weeks leading into summer, Innerbody found that corn was the fastest-rising grilled food in 23 states, including California, New York, Pennsylvania and Wisconsin.

But perhaps the most unexpected finding was that Texas—a state famous for barbecue and meat—ranked among the states with the least grilling interest. Plus, grilled onions took the top spot there! Who would have guessed?!

articles.title: EXCLUSIVE! Author Harlan Coben Reveals Why We’re Obsessed with Netflix’s I Will Find You—And the Secret to the Perfect Plot Twist
articles.content:
在哈兰·科本位于新泽西的家中，你可以找到他全部36本超级畅销悬疑小说。只不过它们的摆放位置和你想象的不太一样。“我通常把它们乱七八糟地堆在办公室里，”他说。“我觉得如果把它们陈列出来，我可能会不喜欢自己，所以它们都在地板上。”但在接受《读者文摘》采访时，他的目光落在其中一本上：一本2023年畅销书《我会找到你》的新平装版，封面上是萨姆·沃辛顿的照片。正如他所说，“它正在称霸榜单。”

哦，确实如此。自6月18日在Netflix上线以来，这部八集剧集已成为今夏最火爆的电视剧，全球观众超过6000万人次（而且还在增加），大家都沉浸在这部扣人心弦的剧集中。沃辛顿饰演大卫·伯勒斯，一个因谋杀幼子而被困监狱的人。当大卫收到证据表明他的儿子可能还活着时，他开始策划越狱，踏上了寻找儿子的征程。不剧透……只能说最后的高潮揭示绝对值得在Reddit上发帖讨论。

“我就是那个玩反转和悬念的家伙，”科本说，他是这部电视剧的执行制片人。“我喜欢玩弄你的预期，让你猜测。但在很多方面，悬念之后的东西才是关键——因为我们看过太多剧集，你一眼就能看出接下来会发生什么。我希望你信任我，看看一切最终会如何收场。”

《我会找到你》是科本自2018年以来与Netflix合作的第13个项目。其他作品还包括《骗过我一次》《想念你》《陌生人》《出逃》等，而最近以他笔下的体育经纪人兼调查员迈伦·博利塔为主角的改编剧已在纽约开拍。这种吸引力并不神秘：科本充满反转的小说通常围绕普通人展开，他们的生活因为一个重大秘密而被颠覆。“悬疑就是让你想翻页或点击下一集，”他说。“我希望我的书和剧集能带来这种体验。”

正在创作下一部小说的科本，带我们走进他写作过程中那条黑暗而曲折的道路，聊聊他喜欢什么样的书，以及为什么《我会找到你》成为今夏的意外爆款。继续往下读，看看为什么我们完全被迷住了。

订阅《读者文摘》的Read Up通讯，每周获取更多娱乐、访谈、幽默、清洁、旅游、科技和有趣的事实。

本次访谈已经过编辑删减和整理。

《读者文摘》：在你的所有图书影视改编作品中，为什么你觉得《我会找到你》特别吸引了如此庞大而狂热的观众群？

哈兰·科本：它拥有观众想要的惊险刺激，尤其是第七集结尾那个令人倒吸冷气的反转。但这部作品的独特之处在于它的情感内核——你真正与萨姆·沃辛顿一起寻找他的孩子。我们中的很多人都会对他的痛苦产生共鸣。如果我们有第二次机会会怎样？我们都想保护自己的孩子，我认为这引起了大家的共鸣。

《读者文摘》：您收到了什么样的反馈？

哈兰·科本：人们真的被感动了，我非常高兴。我收到了很多消息，说人们在结尾时哭了，流下了幸福的泪水。这让我非常开心。

《读者文摘》：总体而言，是什么让哈兰·科本的故事在屏幕上如此成功？

哈兰·科本：当然有反转和悬念，但我认为关键在于情感内核，因为你最终会关心这些人物。这就像我买了世界上最贵的车，但如果里面没有汽油或电力驱动，它哪儿也去不了。在《我会找到你》中，你真的关心萨姆和布里特·洛弗尔饰演的角色，甚至关心米洛·文堤米利亚饰演的角色。

《读者文摘》：作为执行制片人，您参与程度有多深？幕后您在做什么？

哈兰·科本：我深度参与所有作品的所有制作环节。在《我会找到你》中，我重写剧本，与选角导演合作。在主要演员接受角色之前，我与他们每个人都进行了会议。但当我到了片场，看到这些演员和工作人员时，我环顾四周，心想：伙计，我在新泽西的家里有了这个小想法，现在所有这些优秀的人要把它变成现实，送到全世界。那种感觉永远不会过时，你知道吗？

《读者文摘》：我们来聊聊写作吧。关于写一个引人入胜的悬疑故事，人们对写作过程最大的误解是什么？

哈兰·科本：几乎所有方面都是误解。我的下一本书将在九月初出版，名字叫《情节反转：生活、工艺和凌乱的初稿》。这是一本关于写作、生活、工艺和信息的回忆录。我挑战所有写作的神圣法则，说明为什么沉迷于这些规则是错误的。我认为大多数人对整个写作过程有极大的误解。写作是凌乱的，万事都会渗透进来。写故事没有一成不变的公式——如果有的话，我还没找到。

《读者文摘》：你怎么知道自己写出了正确的反转？

哈兰·科本：哦，你不知道。很多都是试错。对于《我会找到你》，我首先想到的是概念。我想写一本从最黑暗的地方开始的书。大卫是一个噩梦般的场景。他觉得自己作为父亲失败了，但现在有了救赎的机会。然后在开始写作之前，我必须知道最后一个反转的答案。这需要很长时间，但我在写第一个字之前就知道结局了。

《读者文摘》：您其他的书也是提前知道结局的吗？

哈兰·科本：是的，我在开始写每本书之前总是知道结局。但有时候我会为Netflix稍微改动一下结局，只是为了戏弄那些读过书以为知道接下来会发生什么的人。

《读者文摘》：令人满意的反转和令人震惊的反转有什么区别？

哈兰·科本：我通常力求两者兼顾。如果我感受到情感共鸣，那通常就是做对了的信号。你想让人惊讶，但也要调动情感。否则，故事就不会起作用，人们也不会向朋友推荐。

《读者文摘》：您的哪本书真正完美收尾了？您有最喜欢的吗？

哈兰·科本：答案总是在变，因为我每天和我的书都有爱恨交织的关系。我真的认为《我会找到你》有最好的反转和结局之一。《出逃》也有很多出人意料的大反转。这两本是我最喜欢的，但我明天可能会改变主意。

《读者文摘》：您床头现在放着什么书？

哈兰·科本：我现在同时在看两本书，这对我来说很不寻常。通常我喜欢一次只看一本。迈克尔·康奈利是老朋友，也是我最喜欢的作家之一，我有他的新小说《铁木》。我还在读莉娜·邓纳姆的回忆录《书呆子》。我女儿告诉我我会喜欢它，当我的孩子们推荐什么东西时，我几乎总是必须看看。

《读者文摘》：为什么悬疑小说成为这么好的夏日读物？

哈兰·科本：我认为它们不只是夏日好读物，它们本身就是好读物。我不把悬疑或推理看作一个类型，因为在这个形式里，我可以做任何事。《我会找到你》是一个关于父与子的故事。我写过救赎、写过失去、写过爱情、写过家庭。你仍然要记得讲故事。如果你列出过去100年你最喜欢的五本小说，我保证至少四本会涉及犯罪。所以我是在一个丰富的传统中写作。

《读者文摘》：考虑到您令人印象深刻的写作速度，采访结束后您会马上回到笔记本电脑前继续写作吗？

哈兰·科本：哦，天哪。我知道这听起来很疯狂，但我总是觉得自己落后了。现在我正在写下一部小说，它将是另一本独立悬疑小说。希望我们结束后我就能开始写了。我需要在这本书上取得更大进展！

在《读者文摘》，我们致力于通过在各自领域具有专业知识和经验的作家创作高质量内容，并与相关合格专家协商。我们依靠包括政府和专业组织、学术机构以及我们作家在适当情况下的个人经历在内的可靠一手资料。我们验证所有事实和数据，用可信的来源支持，并随着时间推移进行回顾，以确保它们保持准确和最新。了解更多关于我们的团队、贡献者和编辑政策的信息。

这篇文章《独家！作家哈兰·科本揭秘我们为何痴迷Netflix的《我会找到你》——以及完美情节反转的秘诀》首次发表于《读者文摘》。
articles.summary:
Somewhere in Harlan Coben’s New Jersey home, you can find all 36 of his mega-popular thriller novels. They’re just not located where you think. “I usually have them piled haphazardly in my office,” he says. “I don’t think I would probably like myself if I displayed them, so they’re on the floor.” But while speaking to Reader’s Digest, he makes eye contact with one, in particular: a new paperback edition of his 2023 bestseller I Will Find You, featuring Sam Worthington on the cover. As he puts it, “It’s dominating.”

Oh, yes, it is. Since its June 18 launch on Netflix, the eight-episode series has become the TV smash of the summer, with more than 60 million viewers worldwide (and counting) hooked on the drama. Worthington stars as David Burroughs, a man languishing in prison for the murder of his young son. When David receives evidence that his boy may still be alive, he embarks on a prison break and goes on a quest to find him. No spoilers … except to say that the climactic reveal is a Reddit thread–worthy stunner.

“I’m your twists-and-turns guy,” says Coben, an executive producer on the show. “I love to play with your expectations and make you guess. But in many ways, it’s not necessarily about the cliff-hanger—it’s about the other side of that cliff-hanger, because we’ve seen all the shows where you can tell what’s going to happen next. I want you to trust me and see how it’s all going to work out.”

I Will Find You marks the author’s 13th project with Netflix since 2018. The list also includes Fool Me Once, Missing You, The Stranger and Run Away, while an adaptation chronicling his sports agent/investigator character Myron Bolitar recently started production in New York City. The appeal is no mystery: Coben’s twist-packed novels are usually built around ordinary people whose lives become upended because of a big secret. “Suspense is making you want to turn the page or click to the next episode,” he says. “I hope my books and shows deliver that.”

Coben, who’s currently working on his next novel, takes us down the dark and windy path of his writing process, what he loves in a book and why I Will Find You became the surprise hit of the summer. Read on to find out why we’re completely hooked.

Get Reader’s Digest’s Read Up newsletter for more entertainment, interviews, humor, cleaning, travel, tech and fun facts all week long.

This interview has been edited for length and clarity.

Reader’s Digest: Of all your book-to-screen adaptations, why do you think I Will Find You, in particular, has drawn such a huge and rabid audience?

Harlan Coben: It has the thrills that people want, especially the gasping twist at the end of Episode 7. But what separates this one is the heart—you’re really with Sam Worthington as he searches for his child. A lot of us empathize with his pain. What if we had this second chance? We all want to protect our children, and I think that resonates.

Reader’s Digest: What kind of feedback have you been receiving?

Harlan Coben: People are genuinely moved, and I’m thrilled by that. I’ve gotten a lot of messages that people were crying and really welling up happily with tears at the end. That makes me very happy.

Reader’s Digest: In general, what makes a Harlan Coben story work so well on the screen?

Harlan Coben: You have the twists and turns, but I do I think it’s the heart, because you end up caring about the people. It’s like I bought the most expensive car in the world, but if it doesn’t have gasoline in it or electricity powering it up, it’s not going anywhere. In I Will Find You, you really care about the characters that Sam and Britt [Lower] and even Milo [Ventimiglia] played.

Reader’s Digest: How hands-on are you as an executive producer? What are you doing behind the scenes?

Harlan Coben: I’m very heavily involved in all aspects of all the production. I was rewriting the scripts and worked with the casting director for I Will Find You. I had meetings with all the major cast members before they took their roles. But when I got to the set and saw this cast and crew, I just looked around and thought, Man, I had this little idea in my house in New Jersey, and now all these wonderful people are going to bring it to life and it’s going to be sent out to the world. That feeling never gets old, you know?

Reader’s Digest: Let’s talk about writing. What’s the biggest misconception about the process of writing a juicy nail-biter?

Harlan Coben: Literally, everything. My next book, which comes out in early September, is called Plot Twist: Life, Craft and the Messy First Draft. It’s a memoir on writing, life, craft and message. I take on all of the sacred cows of writing and say why it’s wrong to get caught up in these rules. I think most people have tremendous misconceptions of how it all works. Writing is messy, and things bleed. There’s no neat formula in the process of writing a story—and if there is one, I haven’t found it.

Reader’s Digest: How do you know when you’ve nailed the right twist?

Harlan Coben: Oh, you don’t. A lot of it is just trial and error. For I Will Find You, the first thing I came up with is the concept. I wanted to write a book that started in the darkest place imaginable. David is a nightmare scenario. He feels that he’s failed as a father but now has a chance at redemption. Then I have to know the answer to the last twist before I start. It takes a long time, but I know the ending before I start the first word.

Reader’s Digest: You know the end in advance for your other books too?

Harlan Coben: Yes, I always know the ending before I start a book. But sometimes I’ve changed the ending a little bit for Netflix, just to mess with the people who have read the book and think they know what’s going to happen.

Reader’s Digest: What’s the difference between a satisfying twist and a shocking one?

Harlan Coben: I’m aiming for both, usually. If I feel the emotional resonance, that’s usually the sign I’ve done it right. You want to surprise but also play with the emotion. Otherwise, the story isn’t going to work and people won’t be telling their friends about it.

Reader’s Digest: Which one of your books really stuck the landing? Do you have a favorite?

Harlan Coben: The answer always changes, because every day, I have love-hate relationships with my books. I really do think I Will Find You has one of the better twists and endings. Run Away also has a number of big twists that no one sees coming. Those are two of my favorites, but I could change my mind tomorrow.

Reader’s Digest: What books are on your nightstand right now?

Harlan Coben: I’m reading two right now, which is unusual for me. Usually, I like one at a time. Michael Connelly is a longtime friend and one of my favorite writers, and I have his new novel, Ironwood. I’m also reading Lena Dunham’s memoir, Famesick. My daughter told me I would like it, and when my kids recommend something, I almost always have to check it out.

Reader’s Digest: Why do thrillers make such great summer reads?

Harlan Coben: I don’t think they make good summer reads; they make good reads, in general. I don’t look at thriller or mystery as a genre because within the form, I can do anything. I Will Find You is a story about fathers and sons. I’ve done redemption, I’ve done loss, I’ve done love, I’ve done family. You still have to remember to tell a story. If you name your favorite five novels from the past 100 years, I guarantee at least four will have a crime in them. So I’m following in a rich tradition.

Reader’s Digest: Given your impressive writing speed, will you go back to your laptop as soon as you’re done with this interview?

Harlan Coben: Oh, man. I know this sounds crazy, but I always feel behind. Right now, I’m working on my next novel, which is going to be another stand-alone thriller. Hopefully I will start writing as soon as we’re done. I need to be further along on it!

Watch the Series!
